# Methods

INTRO

## Research Design

::: content-hidden
Describe the overall design of your study, specifying it as a human subjects study with a between-groups design. Explain why this design is suitable for addressing your research questions.
:::

## Participants

::: content-hidden
Detail the criteria for participant selection, including any inclusion or exclusion criteria. Mention the size of your participant pool and the rationale behind choosing this number. Also, describe the process of recruitment and any compensations or incentives provided.
:::

## Ethical Considerations

::: content-hidden
Since your study involves human subjects, outline the ethical approvals obtained (e.g., Institutional Review Board - IRB approval), and how informed consent was obtained from the participants. Describe any measures taken to ensure the privacy and confidentiality of participant data.
:::

## Experimental Setup

::: content-hidden
Detail the setup of your experiment, including the environment (laboratory setting, real-world setting, etc.), the equipment used (specific AR/MR technologies), and any other materials involved in the training sessions.
:::

## Intervention Description

::: content-hidden
Clearly describe the AR/MR methods used in the training of manufacturing operators. Provide details on the content, duration, and frequency of the training sessions. Explain how these methods are intended to impact the assembly training process.
:::

## Control Group

::: content-hidden
If applicable, describe the control group's conditions, including what traditional training methods were used for comparison against the AR/MR methods.
:::

## Data Collection Methods

::: content-hidden
Elaborate on the types of data collected (quantitative, qualitative, or both), the tools and instruments used for data collection (surveys, performance metrics, observational data, interviews, etc.), and the timing of data collection (before, during, after the intervention).
:::

## Measures and Variables

::: content-hidden
Specify the primary and secondary outcomes measured in your study. Define any dependent and independent variables, including how they were operationalized.
:::

## Data Analysis Plan

::: content-hidden
Describe the statistical or qualitative analysis methods to be used for examining the data. Mention any software or tools that will be utilized for data analysis.
:::

## Pilot Study

::: content-hidden
If a pilot study was conducted prior to the main study, provide details on its purpose, design, and outcomes, and how it informed the main study.
:::

## Limitations

::: content-hidden
Acknowledge any potential limitations or biases in your study design, participant selection, or data collection methods that could impact the interpretation of your results.
:::

## Summary

::: content-hidden
Conclude the methods section with a brief summary that encapsulates the key points of your research methodology, emphasizing its appropriateness and rigor in investigating the efficacy of AR/MR methods for manufacturing operator assembly training.
:::
